itinerary.sim: 3 (Three) Tourist SIM is the simplest tourist option in Denmark — about ₹1,833 for short stays with enough data for maps, ticket apps, and hotspot backup.

Copenhagen.name: Copenhagen
Copenhagen.note: Three days gives you two full city days plus a Roskilde day trip; add a fourth day only if you want a slower museum-and-cafe pace.

activities.name: Nyhavn harbour walk and canal-front photo stops
Strøget shopping street and side-lane design stores
Rundetårn (Round Tower) for city views
Torvehallerne Market snack crawl for lunch or fika
Christiania walk for alternative Copenhagen culture
National Museum of Denmark
Amalienborg Palace square and guard change timing
Rosenborg Castle exterior and King’s Garden walk
Tivoli Gardens in the evening
Morning DSB regional train to Roskilde for an easy day trip from Copenhagen
Roskilde Cathedral
Viking Ship Museum
Back to Copenhagen for a relaxed Nyhavn or Tivoli evening

Aarhus.name: Aarhus
Aarhus.note: Use Aarhus for a compact two-day culture stop with most sights reachable on foot or a short bus ride from the centre.

activities.name: ARoS Aarhus Art Museum and Your rainbow panorama rooftop
Latin Quarter walk for boutiques, cafes, and cobbled lanes
Den Gamle By open-air museum
Moesgaard Museum
Dokk1 and Aarhus waterfront walk
Relaxed evening back in the Latin Quarter

Odense.name: Odense
Odense.note: Odense works well as a one-night stop focused on Hans Christian Andersen heritage and a gentle old-town pace.

days.theme: Hans Christian Andersen Trail & Funen History

activities.name: Hans Christian Andersen Museum
St Knud's Cathedral
Funen Village
Old town walk around central Odense

connections.method: DSB intercity train (Orange fares available) from København H to Aarhus H, about 3 hours
DSB intercity train from Aarhus H to Odense, about 1.5 hours

extras: Expect mid-range Denmark stays to fall roughly in the ₹7,000–₹20,000/night range; book early in Copenhagen for the best value.
Use DOT Tickets for Copenhagen buses, metro, and harbour-area transfers, while Rejseplanen is the best planner for nationwide trains and buses.
Orange train fares on DSB are the cheapest option for Copenhagen → Aarhus and Aarhus → Odense, but they are limited and tied to a specific departure.
MobilePay is useful for local merchants, but international cards are not always fully supported, so keep a backup credit card ready.
Roskilde is the easiest high-value day trip from Copenhagen if you want Viking history without adding another hotel check-in.
Vegetarian travelers should bookmark Riz Raz, use Torvehallerne Market for flexible food stalls, and keep Grød in mind for quick breakfast or porridge bowls.

Odense: Funen Village Odense open air museum
